K039 proton general purpose library                               1925 1451   23

Author: A.J. Koning, IAEA                                         1925 1451   25

Ref.: A.J. Koning, D. Rochman, J.-Ch. Sublet, N. Dzysiuk,         1925 1451   27
      M. Fleming, and S. van der Marck, TENDL: Complete nuclear   1925 1451   28

************** G E N E R A L   I N F O R M A T I O N *************1925 1451   32
                                                                  1925 1451   33
This evaluated data file is based on a software system built      1925 1451   34
around the nuclear model code TALYS [kon07], version 2.0. It is   1925 1451   35
part of a large collection of isotopic evaluations, all created by1925 1451   36
running the system with either adjusted or default input          1925 1451   37
parameters. This means that the data in the entire collection have1925 1451   38
been tested in detail for many isotopes against individual        1925 1451   39
experimental data, while for other isotopes it is only as good as 1925 1451   40
the global quality of TALYS at the moment. The mutual quality of  1925 1451   41
all individual data files is however consistent: The same set of  1925 1451   42
nuclear models is used and, equally important, the same ENDF-6    1925 1451   43
formatting procedures for each isotope. The data file has been    1925 1451   44
created automatically using the ENDF-6 format generator TEFAL.    1925 1451   45
                                                                  1925 1451   46
The resulting data file provides a complete representation of     1925 1451   47
nuclear data needed for transport, activation, damage, heating,   1925 1451   48
radioactivity, and shielding applications over the incident       1925 1451   49
proton energy range from 0.25 to 200. MeV.                        1925 1451   50
                                                                  1925 1451   51
All transport and activation data for neutrons, charged particles,1925 1451   52
photons and residual nuclides are filed. This includes cross      1925 1451   53
sections, angular distributions, double-differential spectra,     1925 1451   54
isomeric production, discrete and continuum photon production     1925 1451   55
cross sections, residual production cross sections and recoils.   1925 1451   56
This general purpose evaluation can thus be used as both          1925 1451   57
transport and activation library.                                 1925 1451   58
                                                                  1925 1451   59
**** N U C L I D E    S P E C I F I C   I N F O R M A T I O N ****1925 1451   60
                                                                  1925 1451   61
None                                                              1925 1451   62
                                                                  1925 1451   63
***************************** T A L Y S **************************1925 1451   64
                                                                  1925 1451   65
TALYS is a computer code system for the prediction and analysis   1925 1451   66
of nuclear reactions. TALYS simulates reactions that involve      1925 1451   67
neutrons, gamma-rays, protons, deuterons, tritons, helions and    1925 1451   68
alpha-particles, in the 1 keV - 200 MeV energy range. This is     1925 1451   69
achieved by implementing a suite of nuclear reaction models into  1925 1451   70
a single code system. This nuclear data file is based on a        1925 1451   71
theoretical analysis that utilizes the optical model, compound    1925 1451   72
nucleus statistical theory, fission, direct reactions and         1925 1451   73
pre-equilibrium processes, in combination with databases and      1925 1451   74
models for nuclear structure. The following output of TALYS is    1925 1451   75
stored in this data file:                                         1925 1451   76
                                                                  1925 1451   77
- Elastic and non-elastic cross sections                          1925 1451   78
- Elastic scattering angular distributions                        1925 1451   79
- Recoil data                                                     1925 1451   80
- Total particle cross sections, e.g. (p,xn), (p,xp),..           1925 1451   81
- Total particle energy spectra                                   1925 1451   82
- Total particle double-differential spectra                      1925 1451   83
- Isomeric and ground state cross sections                        1925 1451   84
- Residual production cross sections                              1925 1451   85
                                                                  1925 1451   86
For a description of the used nuclear models and their            1925 1451   87
parameterization we refer to [kon19]. If for this specific        1925 1451   88
nuclide we deviate from the global parameterization, it is made   1925 1451   89
explicit above under Nuclide Specific Information.                1925 1451   90
                                                                  1925 1451   91
***************** F I L E   I N F O R M A T I O N ****************1925 1451   92
                                                                  1925 1451   93
##### MF1: GENERAL INFORMATION                                    1925 1451   94
                                                                  1925 1451   95
- MT451    : Descriptive data and directory                       1925 1451   96
                                                                  1925 1451   97
This text and the full directory of used MF/MT sections.          1925 1451   98
                                                                  1925 1451   99
##### MF3: REACTION CROSS SECTIONS                                1925 1451  100
                                                                  1925 1451  101
All the data present in the following MT-sections have been       1925 1451  102
calculated with TALYS, possibly normalized to experimental data.  1925 1451  103
If the maximal cross section in an excitation function over the   1925 1451  104
whole energy range does not exceed 1.e-9 b, the MT-number is not  1925 1451  105
included. Cross sections lower than 1.e-20 b are assumed to have  1925 1451  106
no physical meaning and are set to zero. All MT numbers extend up 1925 1451  107
to the highest incident energy.                                   1925 1451  108
The following reaction channels/MT numbers are generally included:1925 1451  109
                                                                  1925 1451  110
- MT2      : Elastic scattering cross section: nuclear +          1925 1451  111
             interference terms                                   1925 1451  112
                                                                  1925 1451  113
Obtained by integrating the "nuclear-plus-interference" angular   1925 1451  114
distributions of MF=6.  Note that because of the interference     1925 1451  115
effect, the tabulations in both MF=6 and MF=3 can be negative at  1925 1451  116
some energies and angles.                                         1925 1451  117
                                                                  1925 1451  118
- MT5      : (p,anything) cross section                           1925 1451  119
                                                                  1925 1451  120
MT5 contains the reactions which can not be stored in any other   1925 1451  121
MT-number. The information of MF3/MT5 can be combined with        1925 1451  122
MF6/MT5 to obtain residual production cross sections particle     1925 1451  123
production cross sections and (double-)differential cross         1925 1451  124
sections.                                                         1925 1451  125
                                                                  1925 1451  126
##### MF6: PRODUCT ENERGY-ANGLE DISTRIBUTIONS                     1925 1451  127
                                                                  1925 1451  128
In MF6 we store all secondary energy, angle, and energy-angle     1925 1451  129
distributions, as well as all residual and discrete + continuum   1925 1451  130
photon production cross sections for channels other than capture  1925 1451  131
(MT102) and reactions to discrete levels (MT51,etc.).             1925 1451  132
All data are generated with TALYS.                                1925 1451  133
                                                                  1925 1451  134
- MT5      : (p,anything) yields and energy-angle distributions   1925 1451  135
                                                                  1925 1451  136
MT5 contains, in this order,                                      1925 1451  137
For neutrons up to alpha particles and photons:                   1925 1451  138
- the production yields per particle for the whole energy range   1925 1451  139
- the secondary energy-angle distributions per particles which are1925 1451  140
  not represented by explicit MT numbers. On a secondary energy   1925 1451  141
  grid the relative emission spectra are given together with the  1925 1451  142
  parameters for the Kalbach systematics for angular              1925 1451  143
  distributions. Inelastic scattering cross sections for discrete 1925 1451  144
  states have been broadened and added to the continuum spectra.  1925 1451  145
For each residual product:                                        1925 1451  146
- the production yields for the whole energy range.               1925 1451  147
- the recoil                                                      1925 1451  148
These distributions can be multiplied with MF3/MT5 to get         1925 1451  149
the production cross sections and (double-)differential cross     1925 1451  150
sections.                                                         1925 1451  151
                                                                  1925 1451  152
##### MF8: RADIOACTIVE DECAY DATA                                 1925 1451  153
                                                                  1925 1451  154
For reactions to isomers, MF8 designates where the information    1925 1451  155
for isomeric versus ground state production, or residual          1925 1451  156
production can be found, i.e. for each MT number it points to     1925 1451  157
either MF6, MF9 or MF10.                                          1925 1451  158
                                                                  1925 1451  159
##### MF33: COVARIANCES OF NEUTRON CROSS SECTIONS                 1925 1451  160
                                                                  1925 1451  161
In MF33, we store the covariances of most, but not all, reaction  1925 1451  162
channels that are given in MF3. All covariances are generated     1925 1451  163
with the TASMAN code [kon19], which generates TALYS runs using    1925 1451  164
a Monte Carlo parameter method.                                   1925 1451  165
For each MT-number, the information consists of:                  1925 1451  166
1. Covariances for MTa, Ea and MTa, Ea (variances)                1925 1451  167
2. Covariances for MTa, Ea and MTa, Eb (correlations for the      1925 1451  168
   same reaction channel between different energies)              1925 1451  169
3. Covariances for MTa, Ea and MTb, Ea (correlations between      1925 1451  170
   different reaction channels for the same energy)               1925 1451  171
4. Covariances for MTa, Ea and MTb, Eb (correlations between      1925 1451  172
   different reaction channels for different energies)            1925 1451  173
Classes 1 and 2 are covered by NI-type subsubsections with        1925 1451  174
flag LB=5, followed by a LB=8 section which contains              1925 1451  175
the cross section variances multiplied by 1.e-3. Next, classes 3  1925 1451  176
and 4 are covered by flag LB=6 to account for the different       1925 1451  177
energy grids that two correlating MT-sections may have. Since     1925 1451  178
the total correlation matrix is fully symmetric, we only need to  1925 1451  179
include the correlation elements between different MT-numbers     1925 1451  180
once, and we do that in the subsection with the lowest of the     1925 1451  181
two MT-numbers. We include covariance information for all the     1925 1451  182
total cross sections, the lumped partial cross sections and the   1925 1451  183
individual inelastic cross sections per level (MT51,...). We do   1925 1451  184
not include covariances for MT600 and higher MT-numbers. For the  1925 1451  185
most important channels, we include cross-channel correlations.   1925 1451  186
                                                                  1925 1451  187
